heading: What's New in 7.0
body:
> **Warning:** 
>
> Breaking Changes in v7.0
>
> This driver version introduces breaking changes. For a list of these changes, see
> the [Version 7.0 Breaking Changes section](node-breaking-changes-v7.0) in the
> Upgrade guide.

The Node.js driver v7.0 release includes the following features:

- Updates the minimum Node.js version to v20.19.0.

- Updates the `bson` and `mongodb-connection-string-url` versions to `7.0.0`.
  To learn more about updated BSON functionality for v7.0.0, see the changes outlined
  in the [BSON v7 release notes](https://github.com/mongodb/js-bson/releases/tag/v7.0.0).

- Updates the peer dependency matrix for the following packages to the following allowed
  ranges:

  - `@aws-sdk/credential-providers`:  ^3.806.0

  - `@mongodb-js/zstd`:       ^2.0.0

  - `gcp-metadata`:   ^7.0.1

  - `socks`:  ^2.8.6

  For the full dependency compatibility table, see the Node.js
  [Component Compatibility](compatibility)
  table on the Driver Compatibility Tables page.

- Improves `MongoClient.connect()` consistency across environments by allowing the
  `connect()` function to run a handshake regardless of whether credentials
  are defined.

- Updates `MongoClient.close()` to attempt to clean up sessions only if the topology supports sessions.

- Marks the options parameter for the `ClientEncryption.rewrapManyDataKey()` method as optional
  to resolve a TypeScript mismatch.

- Wraps `socket.write` calls in a try/catch block to ensure that errors properly result in a retry.

- Adds breaking changes for AWS authentication. To learn more about these breaking changes, see the
  [Version 7.0 Breaking Changes section](node-breaking-changes-v7.0) in the
  Upgrade guide.

- Adds the following error handling improvements:

  - When you drop a collection, the collection returns `false` rather than throwing
    `namespace not found` errors.

  - Aggregation with a `writeConcern` and `explain` throws a `MongoServerError` rather than a
    client-side error.

  - All errors thrown during encryption are subclasses of `MongoError`.

To learn more about this release, see the
[v7.0.0 Release Notes](https://github.com/mongodb/node-mongodb-native/releases/tag/v7.0.0) on
GitHub.
